terms.steward_notes: Final term_id is @bs:data-steward's call. The spec proposes the alphabetic form `BT-IPF-ENDOWMENT-VALUE`; the existing glossary uses numeric IDs (BT-001..BT-118), so the steward may prefer to re-key as BT-119+ alongside the BT-EAD-ATHLETIC-SUBSIDY-RATIO and other BT-IPF-* proposals.

terms.name: Per-FTE Normalization (IPEDS)
terms.definition: The convention for normalizing institution-level financial measures by total full-time-equivalent (FTE) enrollment, producing a per-student figure comparable across institutions of different size. The denominator is `total_fte_enrollment`, computed at Bronze ingest as the NULL-safe sum of three EFIA (12-Month Instructional Activity) component columns: `COALESCE(FTEUG,0) + COALESCE(FTEGD,0) + COALESCE(FTEDPP,0)`. **Critical taxonomy clarification:** the FTE source is **EFIA**, NOT EFFY (which is unduplicated 12-month *headcount*, broken out by `EFFYALEV` — wrong grain) and NOT EF Part A `EFTOTLT` (which is fall-snapshot *headcount* — wrong concept). Spec went through three revisions (v1.0 → v1.1 → v1.3) refining this; EFIA is the locked operative file. Per-FTE values are NULL when either the financial value or FTE is missing or non-positive — **no imputation**. The convention is materialized in `base.ipeds_finance` as `instruction_per_fte`, `institutional_support_per_fte`, and `endowment_per_fte`. Reported FTE columns (`FTEUG`/`FTEGD`/`FTEDPP`) default to NCES *estimated* FTE (`EFTEUG`/`EFTEGD`) when the institution did not provide a reported figure. **EDA-observed (FY23):** `total_fte_enrollment` non-null 97.94%; range 6 (SUNY Empire State College, FY23 transition year) – 135,698; p50 1,514. F1A median FTE 5,461 / F2 median 1,047 / F3 median 504.
terms.source_reference: docs/specs/full-pipeline-ipeds-finance.md §3, §4, §5 (FTE source locked v1.3 to EFIA); governance/eda/full-pipeline-ipeds-finance-raw-eda.md §2, §4 (EFIA grain verified 5,959/5,959; 5-institution spot-check within 1%); IPEDS EFIA dictionary entry (https://nces.ed.gov/ipeds/use-the-data, 12-Month Instructional Activity)
terms.steward_notes: Final term_id is @bs:data-steward's call. The spec proposes the alphabetic form `BT-IPF-PER-FTE`; the existing glossary uses numeric IDs (BT-001..BT-118), so the steward may prefer to re-key as BT-119+ alongside the BT-EAD-ATHLETIC-SUBSIDY-RATIO and other BT-IPF-* proposals. The spec §6 also lists BT-IPF-MARKETING-RATIO and BT-IPF-DATA-COMPLETENESS-TIER as proposed terms (six total); per the doc-generator scope-of-work for this Bronze artifact, only the four bronze-relevant terms (BT-IPF-INSTRUCTION-EXPENSES, BT-IPF-INSTITUTIONAL-SUPPORT-EXPENSES, BT-IPF-ENDOWMENT-VALUE, BT-IPF-PER-FTE) are added in this update — the two derived-signal terms (BT-IPF-MARKETING-RATIO is computed at Silver, BT-IPF-DATA-COMPLETENESS-TIER at Gold) are deferred to silver/gold doc-generator runs.

terms.name: Marketing Ratio (IPEDS)
terms.definition: Ratio of institutional support expenses to instruction expenses. Higher = relatively more spending on administration, marketing, and recruiting vs. teaching. Bounds vary widely; EDA P99 around 5.0. Formula: `institutional_support_expenses / NULLIF(instruction_expenses, 0)`. Computed at silver/base in `derive_marketing_ratio`; passes through verbatim to consumable. NULL when either operand is NULL or when instruction_expenses is 0 (the 34 system-administrative-office rows in FY23 corpus produce NULL by design — that is the correct outcome per spec §5 footnote, not a bug). The arithmetic invariant `marketing_ratio * instruction_expenses ≈ institutional_support_expenses within $1` is enforced by BSE-IPF-010 (silver) and CON-IFP-007 (consumable, carry-forward).
terms.steward_notes: Final term_id is @bs:data-steward's call. The spec proposes the alphabetic form `BT-IPF-MARKETING-RATIO`; the existing glossary uses numeric IDs (BT-001..BT-118), so the steward may prefer to re-key. Definition copied verbatim from spec §6 Business Glossary Terms list. Added in the same commit as the SE-T1/T2/T3 test files per the staff-engineer Q-1 follow-up directive.

terms.name: Data Completeness Tier (IPEDS)
terms.definition: Source-data-completeness signal computed from the count of non-null **independent raw inputs** (`instruction_expenses`, `institutional_support_expenses`, `endowment_value`, `total_fte_enrollment`). Values: `high` (4/4), `medium` (2–3/4), `low` (1/4), `insufficient` (0/4). **This is NOT a CIP→SOC crosswalk-confidence tier** — it measures source-field non-null count, not crosswalk match quality. Renamed from `confidence_tier` in v1.1 to disambiguate. The v1.2 reformulation counts the four independent raw inputs (NOT derived signals like marketing_ratio or per-FTE values) — counting derived fields would double-count expense fields and falsely classify F3 (for-profit) institutions as `high` despite their structural NULL endowment. F3 rows therefore land at `medium` (3/4 max), never `high` — load-bearing invariant verified by 0/277 F3 rows at `high` in the FY23 corpus. Synthesized at consumable in `classify_data_completeness_tier` from base passthroughs; consumed downstream by `raw-ingest-eada.md` for composite-tier fusion.
terms.source_reference: docs/specs/full-pipeline-ipeds-finance.md §6 Business Glossary Terms + §6.2 transformation #2 (v1.2 reformulation); src/gold/ipeds_finance_profile.py::classify_data_completeness_tier; governance/data-dictionaries/consumable-ipeds-finance-profile.md (data_completeness_tier column entry); governance/eda/full-pipeline-ipeds-finance-raw-eda.md §3, §7 (F3 endowment 100% structural NULL)

Definition copied verbatim from spec §6 Business Glossary Terms list (added in the v1.1 patch and reformulated in v1.2). The disambiguation against CIP→SOC crosswalk-confidence tiers is load-bearing for the downstream `raw-ingest-eada.md` spec, which is likely to introduce its own confidence tiers — these two field families must not collide in adjacent consumable tables. terms.name: Aura Score (Institution Brand-Gravity)
terms.definition: A neutral institution-level **brand-gravity** signal expressed as an integer in [1, 10]. Higher = stronger brand presence (endowment + marketing + athletic spend), NOT 'better' or 'worse' — the score does NOT encode a value judgment about whether the brand delivers outcomes; that interpretation emerges only when aura is read alongside ERN and ROI in the FutureProof pentagon (high-aura/low-ERN tells a different story than high-aura/high-ERN). **v1 formula (EDA-finalized 2026-04-30 — see `governance/eda/consumable-institution-aura-eda.md` for the full anchor-validation evidence chain):** for each institution take the population-level rank-percentile of each available input signal (`marketing_ratio`, `endowment_per_fte`, `athletic_spend_per_fte`); drop NULL inputs (no imputation); compute `0.65 · MAX(available_rp_*) + 0.35 · MEAN(available_rp_*)`; rescale via the EDA-pinned P5/P95 percentile bounds (P5=0.1413, P95=0.9400) to [1, 10]; ROUND. The MAX+MEAN composite was selected from 4 candidates after the v0-draft weighted-mean formula failed 11/14 anchor schools (composite-collapse at the tails when three near-equal weights cap any one-signal-extreme school at ~7); v1 passes 13/13 anchors at the §6 `aura_score ≥ 8` threshold (Liberty correctly mid-band at 5 as the moderate-on-all-three control). **`athletic_subsidy_ratio` is deliberately EXCLUDED** from the composite per spec §2 Decision 11 — it encodes a normative judgment (subsidy = 'bad') that overlaps with ROI / ERN and would remove the analytical tension between aura and the rest of the pentagon. Versioned via `aura_score_version` (currently `'v1'`); a future v2 score (e.g., after annual P5/P95 rebound or fourth-signal addition) lands as a new version stamp rather than an in-place rebound.
terms.source_reference: docs/specs/full-pipeline-eada.md §6 Consumable Transformations rule 4 (v1 amendment 2026-04-30); governance/eda/consumable-institution-aura-eda.md (full anchor-validation evidence chain — 14 anchor schools across three classes); src/gold/institution_aura.py (640 lines, lines 93-114 pin the v1 constants WEIGHT_MAX / WEIGHT_MEAN / RAW_SCORE_P5 / RAW_SCORE_P95)
terms.steward_notes: Final term_id is @bs:data-steward's call. The spec proposes the alphabetic form `BT-AUR-AURA-SCORE`; the existing glossary uses numeric IDs (BT-001..BT-118), so the steward may prefer to re-key as BT-119+ alongside the BT-EAD-ATHLETIC-SUBSIDY-RATIO and BT-IPF-* family. **CRITICAL semantic disambiguation:** every downstream consumer (frontend pentagon, MCP tools, comparison specs) MUST surface aura with the 'higher = more brand presence, NOT better' framing. Reading aura as a quality score breaks the analytical contract spec §2 Decision 10/11 establishes. The proposed term spans the bronze/silver/gold conceptual ladder; the v1 finalization 2026-04-30 promoted aura from spec-pending to operational and added the 'paired with aura_score_basis for cross-stratum interpretation' guidance to the definition.

terms.name: Coverage Tier (Institution Aura)
terms.definition: Classification of which upstream sources contributed to a given `consumable.institution_aura` row. 3-value closed enum: `both` (1,492 / 46.3% of FY2023 × 2022 EADA snapshot — reports both IPEDS Finance AND EADA; earns `three_term` aura when endowment is present), `finance_only` (1,183 / 36.7% — reports IPEDS Finance only; non-athletic 4-years like most LACs/SLACs, MIT, Caltech, all for-profits without athletics; earns aura on the `two_term_finance_only`, `two_term_no_endowment`, or `one_term_marketing_only` basis), `athletics_only` (548 / 17.0% — reports EADA only; institutions excluded from Finance by the HD `ICLEVEL=1 AND HLOFFER>=5` filter; aura is NULL for these rows because there is no marketing_ratio signal). Used by downstream consumers to gauge confidence in the composite (an `athletics_only` row has NULL aura by design; a `finance_only` row has aura but on a different basis stratum than a `both` row). **Different from `aura_score_basis`:** coverage_tier records *which sources contributed* (3 values), `aura_score_basis` records *which inputs computed the score* (5 values, including the two endowment-NULL cases that coverage_tier doesn't see). Both columns survive on the consumable schema because they answer different consumer questions. Enforced by CON-AUR-005 P0 (closed enum) and CON-AUR-006 P0 (every row has ≥1 source TRUE).
terms.source_reference: docs/specs/full-pipeline-eada.md §6 Consumable Transformations rule 3 + Consumable Schema; governance/dq-rules/consumable-institution-aura.json CON-AUR-005 / CON-AUR-006
terms.steward_notes: Final term_id is @bs:data-steward's call. The spec proposes the alphabetic form `BT-AUR-COVERAGE-TIER`; the existing glossary uses numeric IDs (BT-001..BT-118), so the steward may prefer to re-key as BT-119+ alongside the BT-EAD-ATHLETIC-SUBSIDY-RATIO and other consumable-zone proposals. The 'different from aura_score_basis' disambiguation is load-bearing for downstream consumers — both columns survive because they answer different consumer questions; conflating them risks downstream stratification regressions.

terms.name: Athletic FTE Source (Methodological Provenance)
terms.definition: Methodological-provenance enum recording which FTE denominator was used to compute the three EADA per-FTE / ratio columns (`athletic_spend_per_fte`, `athletic_revenue_per_fte`, `athletic_subsidy_ratio`) for a given `consumable.institution_aura` row. **3 values + NULL:** `'ipeds_finance'` (1,492 rows in the current snapshot, all `coverage_tier='both'` — IPEDS-Finance annualized FTE; preferred because it matches the denominator used by the Finance-side per-FTE columns in the same row, enabling consistent cross-institution comparison), `'eada_fte_headcount'` (548 rows, all `coverage_tier='athletics_only'` — EADA's own 12-month student headcount, an unduplicated count rather than annualized FTE), `'none'` / NULL (1,183 rows, all `coverage_tier='finance_only'` — no EADA reporting, so no athletic per-FTE values). The two FTE definitions are NOT identical — EDA Item 7 verified `athletic_spend_per_fte` differs ~3× at the median between strata (`ipeds_finance` median $1,990 vs `eada_fte_headcount` median $673). **However**, the 2 × 3 (`aura_score_basis × athletic_fte_source`) interaction grid is structurally degenerate: every aura-computed row uses `'ipeds_finance'` as its FTE source (the 548 `'eada_fte_headcount'` rows are all `coverage_tier='athletics_only'` with NULL aura by construction), so the methodology mix never affects the aura distribution. The column is surfaced for downstream consumers that want to stratify or filter on FTE-methodology consistency outside of aura computation (e.g., a future 'athletics intensity' surface that compares spending across institutions). Introduced via the §5 Option-C amendment 2026-04-30; flagged as a methodological-provenance CDE per spec §6 Data Contract amendment.
terms.source_reference: docs/specs/full-pipeline-eada.md §5 Base Transformations Option-C amendment 2026-04-30 + §6 Consumable Schema CDE candidates list; governance/eda/consumable-institution-aura-eda.md Item 7 (2 × 3 interaction grid degenerate-by-construction analysis); src/silver/eada_base.py (Option-C `fte_source` derivation); src/gold/institution_aura.py (passthrough rename `base.eada.fte_source` → `athletic_fte_source`)
terms.steward_notes: Final term_id is @bs:data-steward's call. The spec proposes the alphabetic form `BT-AUR-FTE-SOURCE`; the existing glossary uses numeric IDs (BT-001..BT-118), so the steward may prefer to re-key. **NEW v1-related term** introduced via the §5 Option-C amendment 2026-04-30 (the spec did not list this term in its initial Business Glossary Terms section because the amendment landed after the term proposal block was written). The 'methodological-provenance enum' framing is load-bearing — the column is NOT a coverage flag (that role is played by has_eada / coverage_tier) and NOT a quality tier (that role is played by aura_score_basis). It records *which FTE denominator was used* so downstream consumers can hedge on methodology consistency.

terms.name: Aura Score Basis (5-Value v1 Enum)
terms.definition: 5-value enum (4 non-NULL + NULL) recording which input set was used to compute the `aura_score` for a given row. **EDA-expanded 2026-04-30 from spec-as-written 3 values → 5 values** after discovering 677 finance reporters with NULL `endowment_per_fte` (mostly for-profits like Phoenix and Grand Canyon, plus IPEDS shell offices); the original 3-value enum did not have a basis to stamp these rows with, and treating their NULL endowment as bottom-percentile (`COALESCE(rp_endowment, 0)`) was the v0-draft bug that drove Phoenix and Grand Canyon down to score 5 — failing the marketing-anchor pass criterion. **Values:** `'three_term'` (1,417 rows; uses marketing + endowment + athletic — the canonical `coverage_tier='both'` flagship case), `'two_term_finance_only'` (579 rows; uses marketing + endowment, no EADA — non-athletic 4-years like MIT and most LACs), `'two_term_no_endowment'` (75 rows; uses marketing + athletic, NULL endowment — for-profits-with-athletics, an unusual structural case), `'one_term_marketing_only'` (573 rows; uses marketing only, NULL endowment AND NULL athletic — for-profits without athletics, IPEDS shell offices), NULL (579 rows; aura_score itself is NULL — 548 `coverage_tier='athletics_only'` rows + 31 zero-instruction-expense edge cases that have `has_ipeds_finance=TRUE` but no usable signal). **The v1 invariant is `aura_score IS NULL ⇔ aura_score_basis IS NULL` (CON-AUR-034 P0)** — this replaces the spec-as-written CON-AUR-011 shorthand `aura_score IS NULL exactly when has_ipeds_finance = FALSE` because the shorthand does not cover the 31 zero-instruction edge cases. **Why this column matters for downstream consumers:** comparing a `three_term` Stanford to a `one_term_marketing_only` Phoenix is **not** apples-to-apples in the same way that comparing two `three_term` flagships is — the basis stamp is the load-bearing signal that lets downstream consumers stratify or hedge cross-stratum aura comparisons. CON-AUR-030 stratifies the bucket-coverage distribution check by `aura_score_basis` (4 strata, ≥4 of 10 buckets per stratum) per EDA Item 7.
terms.source_reference: docs/specs/full-pipeline-eada.md §6 Consumable Transformations rule 4 (5-value enum landed via the v1 amendment 2026-04-30) + Consumable Schema; governance/eda/consumable-institution-aura-eda.md Item 4 (Additional finding requiring spec amendment — 4-value enum expansion from 3 → 4 non-NULL values) + 'v1 Promotion Decision' section; governance/dq-rules/consumable-institution-aura.json CON-AUR-011 / CON-AUR-033 / CON-AUR-034
terms.steward_notes: Final term_id is @bs:data-steward's call. The spec proposes the alphabetic form `BT-AUR-AURA-SCORE-BASIS`; the existing glossary uses numeric IDs (BT-001..BT-118), so the steward may prefer to re-key. **NEW v1-related term** introduced via the EDA-driven 3 → 5 enum expansion 2026-04-30 (the spec did not list this term in its initial Business Glossary Terms section because the EDA expanded the enum after the term proposal block was written; the 5-value enum is now the operational source of truth and CON-AUR-033 P0 enforces the closed domain). The 'load-bearing for cross-stratum comparison' framing is critical — every downstream consumer surfacing aura comparisons (frontend pentagon, MCP tools) must read the basis to avoid apples-to-oranges regressions. CON-AUR-030 stratifies by basis (NOT by athletic_fte_source per EDA Item 7's structurally-degenerate-grid finding).
